mnemonics: B.L.E.A.T. - Remember the key communications criteria for IoT devices: Bandwidth, Latency, Energy, Availability, Throughput.
keyPoints: Bandwidth: The maximum rate of data transfer across a network.
Latency: The time delay in transmitting data packets from source to destination.
Energy Consumption: The power usage of a device, critical for battery-operated IoT sensors.
Availability: The measure of how reliably a network or service is operational.
Throughput: The actual rate of successful message delivery over a communication channel.
commonMistakes: Assuming high bandwidth always means good performance, ignoring latency.
Focusing only on energy efficiency and sacrificing necessary availability or throughput.
Confusing theoretical bandwidth with actual, real-world throughput.
